sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Athletic Club has had mixed results in their last 5 matches with 3 wins and 2 losses, showing inconsistency in defense with 3 games without a clean sheet. Arsenal, on the other hand, is in strong form with 4 wins in their last 5 matches, including dominant performances with high-scoring games (3-0, 5-0).

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their most recent head-to-head encounter on August 9, 2025, Arsenal convincingly defeated Athletic Club 3-0 at home, showcasing their attacking prowess and control with 60% possession and 4 big chances created.

sections.subtitle: Key Statistics
sections.text: Arsenal's ability to score early (first to score in 4/5 recent matches) and their superior expected goals in recent games highlight their attacking threat. Athletic Club struggles defensively at home, conceding in most recent matches, while averaging decent possession and shot creation.

sections.subtitle: Player Impact
sections.text: Arsenal's in-form players like Viktor Gyökeres (4 goals in last 5 matches) and Martín Zubimendi (high ratings and goals) could be decisive. Athletic Club will need to rely on home advantage and tightening their defense to counter Arsenal's attacking momentum.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at San Mamés ended in a goalless draw between Athletic Club and Arsenal. Neither team managed to break the deadlock, despite some promising moments. Arsenal dominated possession and showed more intent in creating chances, while Athletic Club held their ground defensively and looked to counter. Both sides have work to do in the second half to turn opportunities into goals.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Arsenal controlled much of the ball in the first half, frequently entering the final third and creating a notable chance, though they failed to convert. Their attacking play showed more cohesion, and they won more duels overall, indicating a slight edge in physicality. Athletic Club, on the other hand, struggled with possession but managed to make several entries into the penalty area and showed resilience in aerial battles. Their defensive structure has been solid, but they lack the cutting edge in the final third so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half fluctuated between the two sides. Arsenal started with a strong attacking presence, pushing forward consistently in the opening minutes. Athletic Club responded with a significant spell of pressure around the midpoint of the half, showing intent to threaten Arsenal's defense. However, Arsenal regained control for a prolonged period, dominating the latter stages of the half with sustained attacking waves. Towards the very end, Athletic Club mounted a brief resurgence, but it wasn’t enough to shift the balance decisively.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Arsenal, Viktor Gyökeres has been a focal point in attack, coming close to scoring and showing a constant threat in the box. His ability to capitalize on chances will be crucial in the second half. On the Athletic Club side, Iñaki Williams has shown glimpses of danger with his runs, though he needs better service to make a real impact. Both teams will rely on these players to unlock the opposition defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see Arsenal continue their push for a goal, leveraging their possession dominance and looking to exploit any defensive lapses from Athletic Club. Athletic Club, playing at home, will aim to capitalize on counter-attacks and set pieces, using the crowd at San Mamés to fuel their energy. Given Arsenal's higher expected goals in the first half and missed big chance, they seem more likely to break the deadlock. However, Athletic Club’s defensive organization could frustrate them if they remain compact.
